fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Florida Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Florida - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity florida, how to transfer pharmacist license to florida, pharmacist compact florida, florida pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Florida Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Florida Department of Health, Board of Medicine
board.mailing_address:
Department of Health
P.O. Box 6330
Tallahassee, FL 32314-6330

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Florida.

steps.title: Create an Account on DOH MQA Online Services
steps.description: Register as a new user on the Florida Department of Health's DOH MQA Online Services portal using your SSN and Date of Birth.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the DOH MQA Online Services portal, select the Pharmacist license application, and accurately fill out all required sections.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your ACPE-accredited pharmacy school to the Board. If applying by endorsement, provide proof of active licensure in another state and practice history.

steps.title: Complete Background Check & Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Schedule and complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check and register for and pass the Florida Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE).

steps.title: Pass NAPLEX (if by examination)
steps.description: If seeking initial licensure by examination, ensure you have passed the North American Pharmacist Licensure Examination (NAPLEX).

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Florida pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Florida have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Florida. To work in Florida, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Florida?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Florida while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Florida
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license in Florida, navigate to the Florida Department of Health's DOH MQA Online Services portal to register an account and submit your application.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the DOH MQA Online Services portal, click 'Register' in the top right, select 'Health Care Professional', and follow the prompts to create an account using your Social Security Number (SSN) and Date of Birth (DOB).
